title: Regression Streak Loop
summary: Builds confidence by requiring a clean streak of realistic cases and restarting on failure.
steps: Define realistic cases and a target streak length before starting.
Run cases one at a time under consistent conditions and record pass or fail with evidence.
When a case fails, document it, add regression coverage where practical, fix the root cause, and reset the streak.
Do not skip difficult cases to preserve the streak.
After reaching the streak, rerun the highest-risk failures from the log.
stopConditions: target streak achieved and spot checks pass
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Regression Streak Loop for [cases, streak_length, failure_policy].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Define realistic cases and a target streak length before starting.
- Run cases one at a time under consistent conditions and record pass or fail with evidence.
- When a case fails, document it, add regression coverage where practical, fix the root cause, and reset the streak.
- Do not skip difficult cases to preserve the streak.
- After reaching the streak, rerun the highest-risk failures from the log.

Quality gates before completion:
- streak target fixed
- failures reset streak
- regressions added for fixed failures
- evidence recorded

Stop when one of these is true:
- target streak achieved and spot checks pass
- failure pattern requires redesign
- budget ends

Finish with:
- case log
- failure notes
- regression tests
- final streak count

Escalate for human approval before: accepting failed high-risk cases, changing streak length mid-run.
